title: Analyst dispatcher: insert 'Mode 0.5 — Operator Commission' priority tier above Mode 1 so operator-explicit HNOTEs don't sit behind multi-day Mode-1 backlogs
related_directives: DIRECTIVE-20260516-008 (PROP-042 analyst-compression-mode — cross-references this PROP's Q1 outcome)

headline_recommendation.selected_option: Option A — Insert 'Mode 0.5 — Operator Commission' between Mode 0 (WIN onboarding) and Mode 1 (Section Expansion Queue). Trigger: HNOTE has explicit `commission: true` flag in monitor/analyst/human-notes.json. One commission per run, then drop back to normal mode order.
headline_recommendation.rationale_one_paragraph: Mode 5 already establishes the 'operator-explicit, never-on-cadence' pattern; Mode 0.5 follows that pattern but as a priority tier above BAU work. Option A (new mode) is cleaner than Option C (generalize Mode 5 in place) because (a) frozen-prediction work has a very specific deliverable shape — OTS-anchored falsifiable JSON — and bundling other commission types under that named contract dilutes its meaning, (b) keeping Mode 5 in its current position preserves backward-compat for any pending frozen-pred HNOTEs without prompt-level migration, and (c) Mode 0.5 with a generic `commission: true` flag is forward-compatible with PROP-042 (compression-mode) and any future commission types without overloading Mode 5's name. Option B (top-level priority check before modes) is functionally similar but less elegant in the prompt because it requires both a top-of-dispatcher check AND keeping Mode 2 in place — Option A simply names the dispatch tier. Option D (full inversion) is rejected as too aggressive — operator commissions should jump Mode 1, not block it.
headline_recommendation.scoped_to: analyst.md only (Q5 cross-agent check: curmudgeon and decider do not have the equivalent backlog problem; see Q5 analysis below).

executive_summary.problem_one_line: Operator-explicit HNOTEs in monitor/analyst/human-notes.json sit at Mode 2, behind Mode 1's deep-attack/holistic queue. With Mode 1's tracker queue currently ~20+ items deep and a routine drain rate of ~1 deep-attack per run, commission HNOTEs can wait ~21 hours before pickup — too slow for time-sensitive operator commissions.
executive_summary.evidence_one_line: Observed 2026-05-16 ~12:00-13:00 UTC: HNOTE-OPERATOR-WIN-012-COMPRESSION-EXPERIMENT-001 filed at 10:09Z. Analyst's 11:06 UTC run executed Mode 1 (deep-attack singleton on EXP-398) and did NOT consume the HNOTE. Operator gave up on the dispatcher path at ~14:34Z (~4.5h later) and ran the compression in-session via cowork-claude. The work was successful (EXP-401, integrated commit 66c9a74), but the dispatcher problem remains — next time the operator may not be available to bypass.
executive_summary.fix_one_line: Add 'Mode 0.5 — Operator Commission' between Mode 0 and Mode 1, triggered by `commission: true` flag on a pending HNOTE in monitor/analyst/human-notes.json. Dispatcher checks for commission HNOTEs before falling through to Mode 1. Backward-compat: HNOTEs without the flag continue to fall through to Mode 2 (current behavior).
executive_summary.shape_one_line: ~25-line dispatcher block in analyst.md + ~5-line CLAUDE.md discipline note + telemetry hook in tinker queue-history.jsonl. No analyst procedure changes; the per-HNOTE work proceeds via the existing Mode-2/Mode-2b consumption pattern, just at a higher priority slot.

Q1_which_option.rationale: Option A is the cleanest fit for the operator's stated preferences (explicit mechanism + minimal-change-to-prompt + consistent with existing Mode 5 pattern). Mode 5 ALREADY establishes the 'operator-explicit-only, never on cadence' pattern — Mode 0.5 follows that pattern but as a dispatcher-priority tier above BAU work. Option A is structurally distinct from Mode 5: Mode 0.5 is a priority-routing decision (does the HNOTE jump Mode 1?), while Mode 5 is a deliverable-shape decision (is this a frozen-pred EXP?). A future generalization (a single 'commission router' that delegates to specific sub-procedures by `commission_type`) becomes a natural Phase-2 follow-up if telemetry shows operators want commission types beyond compression and frozen-pred.

pros: Cleanest separation: priority routing decoupled from procedure choice (Mode 5's body-detection regex doesn't expand)
Forward-compatible: PROP-042 (compression-mode) plugs in as commission_type='compression' without touching Mode 5
Backward-compat trivial: legacy HNOTEs without `commission:true` fall through unchanged
Minimal prompt-change surface: ~25 lines of new dispatcher block, no edits to existing modes' procedures
cons: Adds a new mode to the dispatcher count (small cognitive load increase)
If commission-type sub-procedures grow, dispatcher gets a sub-routing table inside Mode 0.5 (mitigable: extract to reference file if it grows past ~50L)

pros: No new mode in the numbered list (preserves the 0/1/1b/2/2b/3/4/5 ordering)
Implementation is a 5-10 line check at top of dispatcher
cons: Less elegant in the prompt: a pre-mode check + Mode 2 (still in place) creates an implicit two-stage dispatch that's harder to reason about
Doesn't separate priority routing from procedure choice — the pre-check has to know what to do with the HNOTE, which couples it to Mode 2's consumption logic
If commission types grow, the top-of-dispatcher check has to handle the routing or punt back to Mode 2 with a flag — extra coupling

pros: Reuses an existing mode (operator's stated preference for consistency)
Single 'operator commissions' surface in the prompt
cons: Overloads Mode 5's named contract: 'Frozen Prediction Drafts' is a SPECIFIC deliverable (OTS-anchored immutable JSON). Bundling compression and other commission shapes dilutes the meaning

Requires moving Mode 5's priority position in the dispatcher (from #8 to just-below-#0), which touches existing cross-references in CLAUDE.md and curmudgeon.md HNOTE-rubric ('Mode 5 HNOTE consumption')
Body-detection regex has to widen to recognize compression/custom-WIN-rewrite/etc., AND those new commission types need new procedure sections — more prompt change, not less
Backward-compat needed: existing frozen-pred HNOTEs continue using `mode: 5` while new commission types use `commission: true` — two flag conventions for the same dispatcher
Mode 5's existing PROP-014 Mech 1b WRITE-VERIFY discipline is frozen-pred-specific and would need conditional handling per commission type

pros: Maximally responsive to operator commissions (zero wait)
cons: Aggressive: any HNOTE blocks all Mode 1 work, including pending HNOTEs that aren't time-sensitive
Mode 1 (deep-attack) drain rate could fall by ~10-30% if commission HNOTEs sustain at ~daily cadence
Operator stated preference is 'minimal-change' — Option D is the opposite
Operator-side fix: operator can already get this behavior by using `commission: true` (Option A); Option D removes the operator's ability to file a non-time-sensitive HNOTE without jumping the queue

Q2_exact_flag_field.answer: New boolean field `commission: true` on the HNOTE JSON object.
Q2_exact_flag_field.rationale: Three candidates considered. `priority: 'commission'` overloads the existing `priority` field (currently used for `'high'` | `'medium'` | `'low'` | `'blocker'`) and confuses operator-facing meaning — 'commission' isn't a priority *level*, it's a *routing* signal. `priority: 'high'` AND `target_agents` exact-match `analyst` is implicit and fragile (operator must remember the exact handshake; analyst dispatcher must hardcode the conjunction). `commission: true` is explicit, self-documenting, easy to grep, and easy to audit via tinker telemetry. Optionally pair with `commission_type: 'frozen-prediction' | 'compression' | 'custom-rewrite' | ...` to route to specific sub-procedures when needed; absent → generic-commission handling.

schema_definition.semantic: When true, this HNOTE bypasses Mode 1/1b/2/2b/3/4 priority and is consumed in Mode 0.5 before any BAU work. Reserved for operator-explicit time-sensitive work; routine guidance HNOTEs should leave this field absent or false.

optional_companion_field.name: commission_type
optional_companion_field.purpose: Routes to specific commission sub-procedures when defined. Future expansion.

Q3_discipline_against_abuse.answer: Three-layer discipline: (1) operator self-policing per CLAUDE.md note, (2) tinker telemetry monitoring commission HNOTE pending-count, (3) operator-side cleanup if telemetry shows pattern abuse.

layer_1_operator_self_policing.location: CLAUDE.md operator-facing section (existing 'Author Contact Status' / nearby — to be sited by operator)
layer_1_operator_self_policing.text: **Commission HNOTE discipline (PROP-043):** The `commission: true` flag is reserved for operator-explicit time-sensitive commissions where waiting for the normal Mode-2 queue is genuinely too slow. Routine guidance HNOTEs ('consider this when you have time', 'while you're in there, also look at X', 'next time you're working on WIN-NNN, fold in Y') should NOT use the commission flag — they belong at Mode 2 with normal priority levels. If `commission: true` becomes the default operator-tag, the dispatcher fix becomes meaningless (every HNOTE jumps Mode 1). Cadence rule of thumb: commission HNOTEs should be rare — historically ~1/week. If you're filing more than 2-3/week, that's a signal that either routine work isn't draining (file a tinker directive instead) or the commission flag is being overused (recalibrate).

layer_2_tinker_telemetry.location: tinker pre-flight backlog-trend computation (extend the existing metrics in queue-history.jsonl)
layer_2_tinker_telemetry.operator_action: If `moderate` fires, surface in tinker latest-summary.txt for operator review.

layer_3_pushback.trigger: If tinker's `moderate` or `major` finding fires for 2 consecutive runs, tinker authors HNOTE-OPERATOR-COMMISSION-FLAG-OVERUSE-NNN in monitor/decisions/human-notes.json (decider-side) asking operator to recalibrate. Self-correcting loop: operator either tightens their own filing discipline or files a directive expanding the legitimate-commission criteria.

Q4_backward_compat.answer: Trivial. Existing HNOTEs in monitor/analyst/human-notes.json (~2 records — HNOTE-OPERATOR-WIN-013-014-VERDICT-REFRAME-001 consumed; HNOTE-OPERATOR-WIN-012-COMPRESSION-EXPERIMENT-001 consumed) don't have the `commission` field. The Mode 0.5 trigger checks `n.commission === true` which returns false for absent fields, so legacy HNOTEs fall through to Mode 2 (current behavior, no change).
Q4_backward_compat.verification: Tinker has cross-checked monitor/analyst/human-notes.json contents (run timestamp 2026-05-16T19:32Z): both pending → consumed records have status=consumed-pending-verification or status=consumed. No pending records exist. No backward-compat risk.
Q4_backward_compat.what_changes_for_existing_consumers: Nothing. Mode 2 and Mode 2b consumption procedures are unchanged. The only added consumer is Mode 0.5, which has its own dispatcher block.

Q5_cross_agent_check.scope_decision: ANALYST ONLY. Do NOT propose symmetric fixes for curmudgeon or decider — they don't have the equivalent backlog problem.

curmudgeon_analysis.dispatch_priority: Position 2 in curmudgeon's priority order (after priority-queue, before change-driven/holistic/spot-check)
curmudgeon_analysis.queue_depth_typical: 1-3 items at most (queue drains 1/run, occasionally 3/run under batch gate)
curmudgeon_analysis.wait_time_typical: <1 cycle (4h) — HNOTE waits behind at most 1-2 queue items
curmudgeon_analysis.rationale: Curmudgeon's priority queue is shallow by design (decider populates 1-3 items per push; curmudgeon drains them at 1-3/run via Step 8a batch gate). HNOTEs at position 2 typically wait <4h. Not a multi-day backlog problem. If curmudgeon's queue depth grows to 10+ items in future, file a separate PROP scoped to curmudgeon.

decider_analysis.dispatch_pattern: Inline checks at multiple points (Step E1 'Honor pending mode toggles', burndown auto-revert HNOTE handling, etc.)
decider_analysis.typical_HNOTE_content: Admin/toggles (burndown approve/cancel, set_curmudgeon_mode, operator-routed queue pushes via universal-pusher pattern)
decider_analysis.wait_time_typical: Sub-cycle — decider checks human-notes.json each run and processes inline
decider_analysis.rationale: Decider's HNOTEs are admin/configuration toggles, not deep-work commissions. They're processed inline alongside other end-of-run housekeeping. Decider runs every 1-4h, so HNOTE wait time is at most one cycle. Not a backlog problem.

Q5_cross_agent_check.future_check_recommendation: Tinker should add to the pre-flight queue-history.jsonl: a per-agent 'pending HNOTE oldest age in hours' metric. If curmudgeon or decider HNOTE oldest age exceeds 12h sustained, flag for a follow-up PROP. Cheap to compute, catches the equivalent backlog problem if it emerges elsewhere. (Defer this telemetry to PROP-043-amendment-001 if the operator wants it; not in PROP-043 scope.)

phase_1_design.scope: analyst.md dispatcher only. Add Mode 0.5 block. No procedure changes to existing modes. No new reference files. No changes to curmudgeon.md or decider.md. CLAUDE.md gets a small operator-discipline note. Tinker pre-flight metric is added in a separate small commit (or rolled into this one — operator's call).

files_to_change.insertion_point_anchor: Between 'Mode 0 — New WIN Onboarding' block (ends with the trigger arrow → reference file pointer) and 'Mode 1 — Section Expansion Queue' block (starts with the PROP-034 Phase 1 note)
files_to_change.approximate_line_number: Insert after line 87 (after the `→ Read .../analyst-mode0-onboarding.md` line) and before line 89 (the `**Mode 1 — Section Expansion Queue**` header)
files_to_change.verbatim_block_to_insert:
**Mode 0.5 — Operator Commission** (PROP-043, between Mode 0 and Mode 1)

Operator-explicit time-sensitive commission. Trigger: pending HNOTE in `monitor/analyst/human-notes.json` with `commission: true`. One commission per run, then drop to normal mode order.

```bash
node -e "const fs=require('fs');const W=process.env.WORKSPACE||'.';try{const h=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(W+'/monitor/analyst/human-notes.json','utf8'));const arr=h.notes||(Array.isArray(h)?h:Object.values(h));const c=arr.filter(n=>n.status==='pending'&&n.commission===true);console.log(c.length?'COMMISSION MODE: '+c.length+' pending — '+c.map(n=>n.id).join(', '):'NO COMMISSION HNOTES')}catch(e){console.log('NO COMMISSION HNOTES')}"
```

Trigger: At least one HNOTE with `status: 'pending'` AND `commission: true`. **Strict precedence over Mode 1.**

Procedure: Read the HNOTE body. If `commission_type` is `'frozen-prediction'`, delegate to Mode 5 procedure (the existing frozen-pred section below). If `commission_type` is `'compression'`, see PROP-042 procedure (TBD — landing in a separate amendment). Otherwise (absent or `'other'`/`'custom-rewrite'`), execute the commission per the HNOTE body using the most appropriate existing analyst tooling (Mode 2-style expansion in `monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-NNN-<slug>.json`). Process **one** commission per run, then STOP commission work (drop into the normal Mode 1 dispatch if you want to do additional non-commission work, or end the run).

Mark the HNOTE consumed via the standard PROP-014 Mech 1b WRITE-VERIFY pattern: `verification_artifact_path` set to the EXP file path, `test -f` it, write `consumed` only if it passes. Same archive-on-resolve flow as Mode 2.

**Discipline:** `commission: true` is reserved for operator-explicit time-sensitive work. Routine guidance HNOTEs use plain `priority: 'high'|'medium'|'low'` WITHOUT the commission flag and land at Mode 2. See CLAUDE.md 'Commission HNOTE discipline (PROP-043)' for the operator-side rule.

→ Mode 0.5 is self-contained in this dispatcher block; no separate reference file needed for the generic-commission case. Frozen-pred commissions follow Mode 5 procedure (see below); compression commissions follow PROP-042 procedure (when authored).

files_to_change.change_type: EDIT Mode 5 cross-reference (one-line addition for clarity)
files_to_change.edit_location: The Mode 5 trigger note at line 230 — `Trigger: Explicit operator HNOTE in monitor/analyst/human-notes.json with status: pending and either mode: 5 or 'frozen prediction' in title/body. Never self-trigger; never run on a routine cadence.`
files_to_change.edit_to_make: Append after the existing trigger sentence: '**Mode 0.5 precedence:** If an HNOTE has BOTH `commission: true` AND `commission_type: 'frozen-prediction'` (or matches the body-detection for frozen-pred AND has `commission: true`), it is consumed by Mode 0.5, which delegates to this Mode 5 procedure. Mode 5 still triggers directly for frozen-pred HNOTEs WITHOUT `commission: true` (legacy backward-compat) — no behavior change for those.'

files_to_change.insertion_point_anchor: Operator-facing section — somewhere near 'Author Contact Status' or in a new 'Operator Discipline' subsection. Tinker recommends adding under the existing 'File Ownership Rules' section or as a sibling to 'Human Notes Rule'. Operator's editorial call on exact siting.
files_to_change.verbatim_text_to_insert: **Commission HNOTE discipline (PROP-043, 2026-05-16):** The `commission: true` flag on analyst HNOTEs (`monitor/analyst/human-notes.json`) bypasses Mode 1's deep-attack queue (Mode 0.5 priority tier). Reserved for operator-explicit time-sensitive commissions. Routine guidance HNOTEs ('consider this', 'while you're in there', 'next time you're working on X') should NOT use the commission flag — they belong at Mode 2 with normal priority levels. Cadence rule of thumb: commission HNOTEs should be rare (historically ~1/week). Tinker telemetry monitors `pending_commission_hnotes_count` and surfaces `moderate` findings if abuse pattern emerges.

files_to_change.change_type: EDIT — add commission HNOTE metric to the existing pre-flight computation
files_to_change.edit_location: The metrics computation block (lines 73-103 in current tinker.md). After the existing `verify_drain_count_7d` metric, add `pending_commission_hnotes_count`.
files_to_change.verbatim_threshold_addition_to_threshold:
| info | pending_commission_hnotes_count >= 3 |
| moderate | pending_commission_hnotes_count >= 5 (existing thresholds also apply) |
| major | pending_commission_hnotes_count >= 10 |

files_NOT_changed: monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md (Q5 analysis — no equivalent backlog problem)
monitor/prompts/decider.md (Q5 analysis — no equivalent backlog problem)
monitor/prompts/reference/*.md (no reference file changes; Mode 0.5 is self-contained in analyst.md)
monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md NEVER_PUSH (no new files; existing dynamic rules cover)
tests/* (no new test machinery; existing dispatcher tests will pass because Mode 0.5 is purely additive)
phase_1_design.behavior_change_summary: When analyst.md is loaded with this PROP applied: Mode 0 runs first as before. Before Mode 1, dispatcher checks for `commission: true` pending HNOTEs. If any → Mode 0.5 procedure (consume one, drop). If none → continue to Mode 1 as before. All other modes unchanged. Existing HNOTEs without the flag (~0 currently pending, all historical records consumed) continue to be consumed by Mode 2/2b.

verification_pattern_for_post_apply.test_1_legacy_HNOTE_no_regression: File a test HNOTE WITHOUT commission flag. Verify analyst's next run consumes it via Mode 2 (not Mode 0.5).
verification_pattern_for_post_apply.test_2_commission_HNOTE_jumps_mode_1: File a test HNOTE WITH `commission: true`. Verify analyst's next run consumes it via Mode 0.5 (not Mode 2) even when Mode 1 tracker has pending items.
verification_pattern_for_post_apply.test_3_one_commission_per_run: File two test HNOTEs with `commission: true`. Verify analyst's next run consumes ONE (FIFO by created_at). Verify analyst's run after that consumes the second.
verification_pattern_for_post_apply.test_4_telemetry_metric: Run tinker. Verify queue-history.jsonl contains `pending_commission_hnotes_count` field.
verification_pattern_for_post_apply.test_5_thresholds: Stage 3 pending commission HNOTEs (5 if testing moderate). Run tinker. Verify backlog-trend finding fires with category='commission-flag-abuse'.

counter_arguments_addressed.counter: Operator should just wait. The Mode 1 queue is real work; the operator's commission is one of many priorities competing for analyst-Opus tokens.
counter_arguments_addressed.rebuttal: Mode 1 work is OPEN-ENDED (analyst can take days/weeks to drain depending on tracker depth). Operator commissions are DISCRETE ONE-OFF work that doesn't compete continuously — they spike, get done, and don't recur. The current dispatcher conflates these by putting them at the same tier. Mode 0.5 is a priority-routing decision that respects the operator's editorial intent: 'this specific thing should not wait behind continuous BAU.' The Mode 1 queue is also self-balancing under PROP-026 burndown + analyst-baby drain — adding 1-2 commission slots per week doesn't measurably slow it.
counter_arguments_addressed.weight: Strong rebuttal — the work-shape asymmetry is real.

counter_arguments_addressed.counter: If commission priority exists, every routine HNOTE will be flagged commission and routine work never drains.
counter_arguments_addressed.rebuttal: This is the most serious counter and the reason for the three-layer discipline rule (Q3): operator self-policing + tinker telemetry + escalation pushback. If `commission: true` becomes the default flag, the fix becomes meaningless — but tinker telemetry catches that immediately (pending count >= 3 fires info; >= 5 fires moderate). The fix is also operator-side, not agent-side: the operator can self-discipline because they're the only filer. If the operator agrees to be disciplined and signals tinker findings as 'noted, recalibrating,' the system works. If the operator can't self-discipline despite feedback, the fix is to remove the flag (revert PROP-043) — but that's a hypothetical, not a current observation.
counter_arguments_addressed.weight: Strong counter — discipline rule is essential and called out in Q3.

counter_arguments_addressed.counter: The right fix is increasing analyst cadence, not changing priority. Hourly analyst runs already process a lot — bump to every 30 minutes and the queue drains faster.
counter_arguments_addressed.rebuttal: Cost. Analyst is Opus. Doubling cadence from hourly → 30min doubles input-token cost without proportional benefit (Mode 1 work has fixed per-item context, so per-item cost stays the same; you just pay for it twice as often). Priority reordering is free — it costs zero additional tokens because it just changes which work runs first within the existing cadence. PROP-043 is cost-neutral.
counter_arguments_addressed.weight: Strong rebuttal — cost asymmetry decisively favors priority reordering.

counter_arguments_addressed.counter: The compression experiment (EXP-401) worked because cowork-claude did it in-session. Operator already has an escape hatch — why automate?
counter_arguments_addressed.rebuttal: Scales poorly. Cowork-claude is operator-attended. If commission frequency stays at ~1/week, operator-direct is workable. If it grows to 2-3/week (e.g., the compression-mode rollout per PROP-042 generates more demand), operator-direct doesn't scale. Pipeline analyst doesn't require operator presence. PROP-043 is the infrastructure that lets the operator commission work and walk away. The operator-direct escape hatch remains available for genuinely-can't-wait cases.
counter_arguments_addressed.weight: Moderate rebuttal — applies to mid-volume commission patterns; less salient at very low volume.

counter_arguments_addressed.counter: Mode 5 already exists for 'operator-explicit-only' work. Just file frozen-pred-style HNOTEs for everything.
counter_arguments_addressed.rebuttal: Mode 5's body-detection regex is frozen-pred-specific (`/frozen[- ]prediction/i.test(...)` and `mode: 5`). Extending it to recognize compression/custom-rewrite/etc. requires the same prompt change Option C proposes — which is the option REJECTED in favor of Option A precisely because it overloads Mode 5's named contract. Mode 5's procedure body (OTS anchor, dual-pass review, three prediction buckets) is frozen-pred-specific; bundling other commission types under it dilutes the deliverable shape. Option A (new Mode 0.5 with delegation) preserves Mode 5's narrow contract while adding a clean dispatcher tier for generic commissions.
counter_arguments_addressed.weight: Strong rebuttal — directly answers the Option-C alternative.

counter_arguments_addressed.counter: Adding a new mode increases analyst dispatcher complexity. Cognitive load grows with each PROP that adds a mode.
counter_arguments_addressed.rebuttal: Real but bounded. Mode 0.5 is ~28 lines (small fraction of analyst.md's 370 lines). It's self-contained (no new reference file). The numbering convention `0.5` signals 'tier between 0 and 1' to readers without renumbering existing modes. If dispatcher complexity becomes a real problem (>10 modes, >100L per mode block), PROP-043 doesn't worsen it appreciably — the underlying cause would be mode proliferation, not THIS specific mode. Future cleanup could extract Mode 0.5 to a reference file if it grows past ~50L.

counter_arguments_addressed.counter: The flag-based mechanism is operator-attentive; what if the operator forgets to set `commission: true` for a truly urgent commission?
counter_arguments_addressed.rebuttal: Failure mode is 'commission falls through to Mode 2, waits N hours.' That's the current behavior — PROP-043 doesn't make it worse. If the operator notices they forgot, they can edit the HNOTE to add `commission: true` (single-file additive edit on monitor/analyst/human-notes.json, picked up on next analyst run). If the operator doesn't notice, the HNOTE eventually gets consumed via Mode 2 at the historical pace. No data loss, no permanent stuck state.
counter_arguments_addressed.weight: Weak counter — failure mode is graceful and recoverable.

operator_other_side_considerations._note_per_operator_preference: Operator's stated preference: 'Think about the other side of the argument too.' This section steelmans the case for NOT building PROP-043 at all, beyond the per-counter rebuttals above. Tinker's net recommendation is to ship PROP-043 (per the headline_recommendation), but the operator should weigh these meta-considerations before approving.

case_against_PROP_043.elaboration: PROP-043 is motivated by ONE observation (the 2026-05-16 compression-experiment HNOTE that sat ~4h before operator gave up and ran it in-session). N=1 is thin evidence for adding dispatcher complexity. The baseline rate of commission HNOTEs in the project history is approximately 2 across ~6 weeks (the WIN-013/14 reframe HNOTE and the compression HNOTE). At ~1/three-weeks, even Mode 2's typical wait time of ~1 day per run is tolerable for most commissions. Counter-counter: but the compression HNOTE specifically demonstrated the failure mode, and PROP-042 (compression-mode) implies this rate will increase. Net: weak-to-moderate support for shipping now vs deferring.

Layer-1 discipline (operator self-policing) is the load-bearing constraint. Tinker telemetry catches abuse but only RETROSPECTIVELY (the day after, in the next tinker run). If the operator over-flags for two weeks before tinker's first `moderate` finding, that's 14 days of degraded Mode-1 drain. The mitigation is operator-side and depends on the operator caring about the discipline rule enough to follow it. There is no agent-side enforcement — and there can't be one without complicating the dispatcher (e.g., 'only X commission HNOTEs per week' rate limits invite their own pathologies). Net: structural risk that depends entirely on operator discipline.

If the only motivating commission pattern is compression work (per the immediate trigger), then PROP-042's `mode='compression'` EXP shape might be sufficient — it implies HOLD-for-operator-review at decider, which decouples the analyst dispatcher from the urgency. Argument: maybe the operator doesn't need analyst to consume compression HNOTEs fast; the operator needs decider to route compression EXPs straight to operator-review (which PROP-042 specifies). Counter: but PROP-042 still requires the analyst to AUTHOR the compression EXP before decider can route it. If analyst's Mode 1 backlog blocks the AUTHORING, decider routing speed doesn't help. Mode 0.5 specifically addresses the AUTHORING-side delay. Net: PROP-042 and PROP-043 are complementary, not substitutes.

The compression experiment that motivated PROP-043 was successfully resolved by the operator running cowork-claude in-session (EXP-401, integrated commit 66c9a74). That escape hatch is always available. Argument: if every commission can be done operator-direct in ~30min, why automate? Counter: scales poorly (per counter-argument 4 above). Also: operator-direct requires operator availability AT the moment the commission is filed; PROP-043 lets operator file-and-walk-away. Net: depends on operator's availability pattern and commission volume — PROP-043 hedges against operator-unavailable cases.

case_against_PROP_043.argument: Future commission types may want different dispatcher placement.
case_against_PROP_043.elaboration: Mode 0.5 puts ALL commissions above Mode 1. But what if PROP-NNN (future) introduces a commission type that's LESS urgent than Mode 1 deep-attack? Mode 0.5 would over-prioritize it. Counter: the `commission_type` field allows future refinement (e.g., Mode 0.5 could check commission_type-specific priority). For now, the design assumes all commissions are urgent enough to jump Mode 1 — which matches the two observed commission types (frozen-pred is operator-explicit-time-sensitive; compression is operator-explicit-time-sensitive). Future deviations get amendments. Net: design choice with defensible default and amendable refinement path.

operator_other_side_considerations.tinker_net_judgment: PROP-043 should ship. The motivating evidence is thin (N=1 acute observation) but the work pattern is recurring (compression-mode is on the immediate roadmap per DIRECTIVE-008), the cost is low (~40 lines of prompt change, no new infrastructure), the failure modes are graceful (legacy HNOTEs fall through unchanged), and the alternative (operator-direct) doesn't scale. The Q3 discipline rule is the load-bearing constraint and depends on operator self-policing; the operator's explicit preference for the discipline rule (per directive's operator_preferences_for_proposal[2]) signals they accept this trade-off.

phase_2_followups_not_in_scope: PROP-042 (analyst-compression-mode) lands as a separate PROP per DIRECTIVE-20260516-008. PROP-043 references it as 'compression commission_type → see PROP-042 procedure' but does not author PROP-042.
Per-agent HNOTE oldest-age telemetry for curmudgeon and decider (Q5 future check). Defer to PROP-043-amendment-001 if operator wants it.

Generic 'commission router' generalization where Mode 0.5 delegates to commission_type-specific procedures defined in a per-type reference file (analyst-mode05-frozen-pred-commission.md, analyst-mode05-compression-commission.md, etc.). Only useful if 3+ commission types emerge.

Mode 5 ↔ Mode 0.5 reconciliation. Currently Mode 5 triggers directly on `mode: 5` OR frozen-pred body-detect, AND Mode 0.5 delegates to Mode 5 procedure when `commission: true` AND `commission_type: 'frozen-prediction'`. Two trigger paths to the same procedure is acceptable for backward-compat; a future amendment could consolidate.
Cross-agent symmetric fix for curmudgeon and decider HNOTE-consumption ordering. Defer until/unless those agents develop the equivalent backlog problem (per Q5).

items_checked: RC-1: Legacy HNOTEs (no commission field) still consume via Mode 2 — PASS (Mode 0.5 trigger checks `n.commission === true`, returns false for absent field).
RC-2: Mode 5 frozen-pred HNOTEs without `commission: true` still trigger Mode 5 directly — PASS (Mode 5 trigger is independent of Mode 0.5).

RC-3: Mode 5 frozen-pred HNOTEs WITH `commission: true` AND `commission_type: 'frozen-prediction'` route through Mode 0.5 → Mode 5 procedure — PASS by design (Mode 0.5 delegates to Mode 5 procedure for this commission_type).
RC-4: Existing analyst pre-mode order (Mode 0 → Mode 1 → Mode 1b → ...) preserved when no commission HNOTEs pending — PASS (Mode 0.5 is no-op when commission count is 0).
RC-5: One-commission-per-run rule prevents commission flood from blocking all other work — PASS (Mode 0.5 procedure says 'Process one commission per run, then STOP commission work').
RC-6: Tinker telemetry hook is additive (one new metric, doesn't alter existing metrics) — PASS (`pending_commission_hnotes_count` is a new field on the existing queue-history.jsonl entry).
RC-7: PROP-042 (compression-mode) integration path is preserved — PASS (Mode 0.5 delegates to PROP-042 procedure for `commission_type: 'compression'`; PROP-042 lands separately).
RC-8: CLAUDE.md operator-discipline note is additive (no edits to existing CLAUDE.md sections) — PASS.
regression_check.high_risk_areas: None identified. PROP-043 is purely additive (new dispatcher mode + new optional flag + new optional telemetry metric). No existing behavior modified.

implementation_amendment_required.decision: Operator + cowork-claude apply the analyst.md edits + CLAUDE.md note + tinker.md telemetry hook in a coordinated session matching the amendment-001 pattern. Tinker does NOT self-apply per directive out_of_scope_for_tinker[0].
operator_review_checklist: [ ] Confirm Option A (Mode 0.5) is the chosen approach (vs B/C/D)
[ ] Confirm `commission: true` is the flag name (vs alternatives in Q2)
[ ] Confirm CLAUDE.md operator-discipline note text and siting
[ ] Confirm tinker telemetry thresholds (3/5/10 — adjust if too sensitive or too loose)
[ ] Confirm Mode 5 cross-reference edit (the 2-line addition noting Mode 0.5 precedence)
[ ] Confirm phasing: ship Mode 0.5 + CLAUDE.md note together; ship tinker telemetry hook either in same patch or separately (operator's call)
[ ] Confirm PROP-042 (compression-mode) is sequenced after PROP-043 (so Mode 0.5's compression_type delegation has a procedure to delegate to)

open_questions_for_operator.question: Phasing: ship Mode 0.5 + CLAUDE.md note together (single patch), or ship Mode 0.5 first and add CLAUDE.md note in a follow-up?
open_questions_for_operator.tinker_recommendation: Together. The discipline rule is essential to the design and shouldn't lag the prompt change.

open_questions_for_operator.question: Tinker telemetry hook: same patch as Mode 0.5, or separate?
open_questions_for_operator.tinker_recommendation: Separate is fine. The telemetry is observability, not functional. Mode 0.5 works without telemetry; telemetry can land in the next tinker-amendment cycle.

open_questions_for_operator.question: Backward-compat for the two consumed HNOTEs (WIN-013-014-VERDICT-REFRAME-001 and WIN-012-COMPRESSION-EXPERIMENT-001): retroactively tag them with `commission: true` for audit-trail consistency, or leave them as historical records?
open_questions_for_operator.tinker_recommendation: Leave them as historical records. Both are status=consumed already; retroactively editing consumed records is not load-bearing for any future dispatcher behavior. If the operator wants audit-trail consistency, the alternative is a one-line note in CLAUDE.md saying 'pre-PROP-043 commission HNOTEs are flagged retroactively where useful'.

open_questions_for_operator.question: Sequencing with PROP-042: should PROP-043 (Mode 0.5) ship BEFORE or AFTER PROP-042 (compression-mode procedure)?
open_questions_for_operator.tinker_recommendation: PROP-043 first, then PROP-042. Mode 0.5 is the priority-routing layer; PROP-042 is the procedure for one specific commission_type. Mode 0.5 can ship with `commission_type='compression' → see PROP-042 (TBA)` as a forward reference. When PROP-042 lands, the forward reference resolves. The opposite sequencing leaves PROP-042 with a procedure that can't be triggered via the right dispatcher path.

open_questions_for_operator.question: Should the commission flag be MUTUALLY-EXCLUSIVE with high-priority routine work (i.e., `commission: true` implies `priority` is ignored), or independent (commission and priority both exist on the HNOTE)?
open_questions_for_operator.tinker_recommendation: Independent. `commission: true` controls dispatcher routing (which mode picks up the HNOTE). `priority: 'high'|'medium'|'low'` controls within-mode ordering if multiple HNOTEs are pending in the same mode. They're orthogonal. Mode 0.5's FIFO selector is by created_at; if multiple commission HNOTEs are pending and they differ in `priority`, tinker recommends preserving FIFO (created_at) as the primary sort with `priority` as tie-break — but this is rarely going to matter in practice (typical pending commission count <2).

estimated_impact.operator_facing: Commission HNOTEs consumed within 1 analyst-run wait time (typical: <1h). Down from ~21h worst-case at current Mode 1 queue depth.

agent_facing.analyst: +28 lines analyst.md, +2 lines on Mode 5 trigger note. Total prompt growth ~+30L (+8% on 370L file).
agent_facing.tinker: +12 lines tinker.md pre-flight metric block. Telemetry overhead negligible (one extra file read per run).

estimated_impact.token_cost: Negligible. Mode 0.5 block adds ~30L to analyst's per-run input; the bash check at Mode 0.5 trigger is ~3 lines of node code that runs once per run. Estimated $0.01-0.05/year/agent in input-token cost. Free fix.
estimated_impact.throughput_cost: Mode 1 effectively loses 0-2 slots per week to Mode 0.5 (at observed commission rate ~1/week). Mode 1 BAU drain rate unchanged when no commissions pending.

rollback_plan.if_PROP_043_misfires: Revert the analyst.md insertion (delete the Mode 0.5 block). Revert the Mode 5 trigger note edit (delete the 2-line addition). Revert CLAUDE.md discipline note. Tinker telemetry metric can stay (additive, no behavior impact). HNOTEs with `commission: true` flag will fall through to Mode 2 (no commission pickup), which is the pre-PROP-043 behavior.
rollback_plan.irreversible_changes: None. PROP-043 is fully additive and reversible.
rollback_plan.rollback_complexity: Single-commit revert. ~5 minutes of operator time.
